documentation:
Opens your editor to reorder, squash, reword, or drop commits. After saving, Git replays commits. Resolve conflicts with git add then git rebase --continue.

Example:
  git rebase -i origin/main

summary: Apply one commit onto current branch
documentation:
Takes the patch from a single commit and applies it on HEAD — useful for backporting fixes without merging whole branches.

If conflicts: fix files, git add, git cherry-pick --continue.

summary: Binary search for a bad commit
documentation:
After git bisect start, mark commits as good/bad until Git finds the first bad commit. End with git bisect reset.

Use when a regression appeared between two known-good commits.
Lets you check out another branch in a separate directory without stashing — ideal for urgent hotfixes while staying on feature work.
